subject: PATIENT
heading: BRIEF HOSPITAL COURSE:__page_no__3
text: brief hospital course: [patient_name] was admitted on [encounter_date] for congestion, cough, and shortness of breath upon laying down. upon admission, her respiratory pcr panel and 2 sets of blood cultures were negative. patient was started on empiric iv vancomycin from [encounter_date] to [encounter_date] and zosyn from [encounter_date] to [encounter_date]. she underwent a bronchoscopy washout that was notable for copious thick yellow secretions throughout airway. her bronch cultures was positive for aspergillus antigen 0.86, but no other growth from cultures. during this hospitalization, she was started on nebulizer treatments and cpt to assist with airway clearance given the appearance of her bronchoscopy. given her recurring infections, her cyclosporine goal was adjusted to 75-125. a ct chest was also completed on [encounter_date] which appeared stable. on [encounter_date], her spirometry was repeated which showed an improvement in her fev1 1.72 from 1.52. in regards to her nausea, it improved after holding her mycophenolate. no emesis during this hospitalization. an mri cervical, lumbar, and thoracic spine was completed for pain medicine to further evaluate her ongoing chronic lower back pain which was properly managed with a multi modal approach. the patient was evaluated on the day of discharge, and she is medically stable for discharge

text: brief hospital course: [patient_name] was admitted on [encounter_date] for congestion, cough, and shortness of breath upon laying down. upon admission, her respiratory pcr panel and 2 sets of blood cultures were negative. patient was started on empiric iv vancomycin from [encounter_date] to [encounter_date] and zosyn from [encounter_date] to [encounter_date]. she underwent a bronchoscopy washout that was notable for copious thick yellow secretions throughout airway. her bronch cultures was positive for aspergillus antigen 0.86, but no other growth from cultures. during this hospitalization, she was started on nebulizer treatments and cpt to assist with airway clearance given the appearance of her bronchoscopy. given her recurring infections, her cyclosporine goal was adjusted to 75-125. a ct chest was also completed on [encounter_date] which appeared stable. on [encounter_date], her spirometry was repeated which showed an improvement in her fev1 1.72 from 1.52. in regards to her nausea, it improved after holding her mycophenolate. no emesis during this hospitalization. an mri cervical, lumbar, and thoracic spine was completed for pain medicine to further evaluate her ongoing chronic lower back pain which was properly managed with a multi modal approach. the patient was evaluated on the day of discharge, and she is medically stable for discharge
